title: [ExportVerilog] Add caseInsensitiveKeywords Lowering Options
body:
Add a new LoweringOption, `caseInsensitiveKeywords`.  When set, this will
cause ExportVerilog to mangle names which conflict with keywords case
insensitively.  This exists to avoid lint warnings which error about this.

As a prerequisite, first plumb a constant reference to LoweringOptions around inside ExportVerilog and its utilities.

This is done to address linters which choose to warn about case insensitive Verilog keyword collisions.  E.g., some linters will complain about `REG` and `Reg` even though they aren't actually Verilog keywords.

### Example

Input:
```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input REG: UInt<1>
    output Reg: UInt<1>

    connect Reg, REG
```

Output with `firtool Foo.fir`:
```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.52.0-97-g848f35b3f
module Foo(
  input  REG,
  output Reg
);

  assign Reg = REG;
endmodule
```

Output with `firtool firrtl-snippets/KeywordCollision.fir -lowering-options=caseInsensitiveKeywords`:
```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.52.0-97-g848f35b3f
module Foo(
  input  REG_0,
  output Reg_0
);

  assign Reg_0 = REG_0;
endmodule
```
